Patient Handout

Medication Instructions

Vaginal & Vulvar Estrogen Cream

How to apply estrogen cream correctly — and why continuing long-term is key to lasting benefit.

Why Use Vaginal Estrogen Cream?

Restores thickness and flexibility to vaginal tissue
Relieves dryness, burning, and pain with intercourse
Lowers risk of urinary tract infections
Works locally with very little absorption into the body

For ongoing benefit, continue use long term. Vaginal estrogen stays effective only while you use it — benefits fade if you stop.

Method 1 — Vulvar Application

Step-by-Step

  1. 1
    Wash and dry your hands thoroughly.
  2. 2
    Squeeze the tube to express ½ gram of cream — enough to cover the top half of your index finger.
  3. 3
    Locate the vaginal opening. Just above it is the urethra (the small opening where urine exits — use the diagram below to find it).
  4. 4
    Carefully spread the cream around the vaginal opening and urethral area. Make sure to cover the opening and just inside the vagina, where most estrogen receptors are located.

Method 2 — Vaginal Application (with applicator)

  1. 1
    Screw the applicator onto the tube and fill to the prescribed dose (1–2 grams).
  2. 2
    Insert the applicator like a tampon — lie on your back with knees bent, or stand with one foot on a chair.
  3. 3
    Press the plunger all the way until it stops, then remove the applicator.
  4. 4
    Wash the applicator with warm, soapy water and rinse well. Tip: single-use applicators are available on Amazon — search "vaginal estrogen applicator."

Method 3 — Vaginal Application (without applicator)

  1. 1
    Place 1 gram of cream on your fingertip.
  2. 2
    Insert your finger into the vagina.
  3. 3
    Spread the cream on the front wall of the vagina (closest to your bladder and urethra).
